title: Review & setlist: Charlie Puth turns MGM Music Hall into a masterclass in musicianship
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Charlie Puth performed at MGM Music Hall in Fenway, blending jazz and pop from his 'Whatever's Clever!' album.
Puth's concert showcased his musicianship with improvisations, vocal control, and interactions involving music theory discussions.
Openers Ally Salort and Daniel Seavey set the tone with indie-pop and live-looping performances, engaging the audience effectively.

title: Review and setlist: Jakob Dylan’s choice to channel Tom Petty pays off at Beverly Wallflowers show
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: The Wallflowers played 'Bringing Down the Horse' in full at The Cabot in Beverly, celebrating the album's 30th anniversary.
Following their set, the band performed Tom Petty's 'Long After Dark' in its entirety, showcasing a mix of rock classics.
Jakob Dylan's performance highlighted both his unique style and his ability to interpret Petty's work with enthusiasm and respect.

title: Here’s where people are moving — and leaving — in Mass., according to new U.S. Census data
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston experienced the largest population decline in Massachusetts, with a loss of 1,338 residents last year.
Everett and Worcester saw the biggest population gains, adding 2,362 and 1,916 residents respectively, according to U.S. Census data.
High housing costs and decreased immigration are contributing factors to population declines in Massachusetts cities.

title: These were the Mass. companies on the new Forbes list of largest family businesses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Fidelity Investments ranks No. 14 and Demoulas Super Markets No. 69 on Forbes' largest family businesses list.
Fidelity earns $37.7 billion annually; Demoulas Super Markets makes $8 billion, according to the Forbes report.
Fidelity is 49% owned by the Johnsons, while the Demoulas family owns 100% of their supermarket chain.

title: UMass Amherst launches major housing revitalization project
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: UMass Amherst partners with American Campus Communities for major housing overhaul to modernize student accommodation.
The project will phase renovations to keep student housing affordable and improve amenities amid rising enrollment.
The plan aligns with Massachusetts' housing priorities, addressing shortages and high living costs across the state.

title: Review & Setlist: Lucinda Williams brings protest songs, fighting spirit to Medford
author: Terence Cawley
bullets: Lucinda Williams performed at the Chevalier Theatre in Medford on May 22, featuring songs from her latest album 'World’s Gone Wrong'.
Williams incorporated protest songs into her setlist, with eight of the next nine songs from her 2020 and 2026 albums.
The concert concluded with covers of Neil Young's 'Rockin’ in the Free World' and Skip James' 'Hard Time Killin’ Floor Blues'.

title: Twins unload on Justin Slaten as Red Sox lose 8-6 at Fenway Park
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Justin Slaten gave up four runs, including two home runs, in the seventh inning loss to the Twins.
Payton Tolle started strong for Boston with nine strikeouts but was pulled after six innings at 85 pitches.
Despite Boston scoring six runs, Minnesota Twins secured an 8-6 victory with an additional insurance run in the ninth.

title: Roger Clemens throws out first pitch at Fenway Park to son, Twins player Kody Clemens
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roger Clemens threw the first pitch at Fenway Park to his son, Kody Clemens, before a Red Sox-Twins game.
Clemens, wearing a No. 21 'Rocket Man' jersey, was honored with a bobblehead giveaway at the event.
Kody Clemens, now with the Twins, previously played for the Detroit Tigers and Philadelphia Phillies since 2022.

title: Celtics’ Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Derrick White named to 2025-26 NBA All-Defensive First Team with 58 votes.
White recorded a career-high 98 blocks and 88 steals for the Boston Celtics.
Celtics finished 56-26 in regular season, despite first-round playoff exit.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: "Glory" is available on Netflix until June 1, featuring Denzel Washington's Oscar-winning performance in a Civil War drama.
"Arco", nominated for Best Animated Feature, follows a boy time-traveling to 2075. It's streaming on Hulu.
"Maximum Pleasure Guaranteed" on Apple TV stars Tatiana Maslany as a suburbanite embroiled in crime and intrigue.
